title: Lomatia hirsuta subsp. obliqua

herb_names_common.name: hooked mountain pepperbush (lomatia hirsuta subsp. obliqua)

what_description: Lomatia hirsuta subsp. obliqua is a subspecies of the Lomatia hirsuta species, belonging to the family Proteaceae. It is an evergreen shrub with a woody stem, 0.5-1.5 meters in height, with a broad, lanceolate leaf shape and a length of 5-15 cm, with a hairy surface. The leaves are arranged oppositely on the stem and have a cuneate base. The plant produces solitary, axillary inflorescences with a single white flower, 3-4 cm in diameter, with a four-lobed calyx and a corolla with four petals, which are notched at the apex. The inflorescences are produced in the axils of the leaves, in the summer months.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Lomatia hirsuta subsp. obliqua are inflammation, fever and rheumatism, which it treats by reducing pro-inflammatory compounds and alleviating pain. Additionally, it is used to alleviate symptoms of asthma, including bronchial constriction, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis by acting as an expectorant and anti-inflammatory. Lomatia hirsuta subsp. obliqua also exhibits antidiarrheal properties, reducing the frequency and severity of diarrhea. Furthermore, it has been shown to treat digestive issues, including gastroenteritis, by reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms. Its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties make it effective in treating wounds, infections, rheumatoid arthritis, diabetes, migraines, and hypertension, while its anti-allergic properties alleviate eczema and anxiety.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Lomatia hirsuta subsp. obliqua are its antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the flavonoids, phenolic acids, and ascorbic acid present in the plant, helping to protect cells from oxidative damage. The bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, also exhibit antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of various microorganisms. Additionally, Lomatia hirsuta subsp. obliqua displays anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, which may reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The plant's analgesic properties are linked to the action of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds, which may help to reduce pain by modulating the nervous system. Furthermore, the antifungal properties of Lomatia hirsuta subsp. obliqua are attributed to the sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds, which may inhibit the growth of various fungal pathogens.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Lomatia hirsuta subsp. obliqua are lomatium alkaloids, which have been shown to possess cytotoxic and anti-inflammatory properties, phenolic acids such as ferulic acid and sinapic acid, which exhibit antioxidant activity and antimicrobial effects, flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, which display anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, terpenoids including sesquiterpenes and diterpenes, which have been found to possess antimicrobial and antifungal activities, and saponins, which have been reported to show hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory effects.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Lomatia hirsuta subsp. obliqua are infusion, tincture, tea, salve, and decoction. Infusions and teas are made by steeping the leaves in hot water, which is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, used traditionally for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Tinctures are concentrated extracts of the plant in a solvent, such as ethanol or glycerol, used for their sedative and anti-anxiety effects. A salve is made by infusing the leaves in oil, which is applied topically for its anti-inflammatory properties, used traditionally for treating skin issues and wounds. Decoctions are made by boiling the leaves and roots in water, used traditionally for their expectorant properties, to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis.
side_effects_description: The possible side effect of using Lomatia hirsuta subsp. obliqua are gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea, nausea and vomiting due to the presence of pyrrolizidine alkaloids which can cause hepatotoxicity and gastrointestinal irritation when ingested in large amounts. This is because these alkaloids can damage the lining of the digestive tract and cause inflammation leading to the symptoms mentioned above. The liver may also be affected as pyrrolizidine alkaloids can cause liver damage and even liver failure in severe cases. The exact mechanism of action is through the biotransformation of the alkaloids into pyrrolizidine alkaloid N-oxides which are highly toxic to the liver and gastrointestinal system. This can lead to the induction of vomiting, nausea, and diarrhea as the body tries to eliminate the toxin.
